weather.summary: Saturday: 10°C, few clouds. Sunday: 10°C, overcast clouds

metadata.greeting: Get ready for a weekend of fun! I've found some fantastic activities in Hackney and beyond, with a mix of indoor and outdoor options to suit all interests. Plus, there are some great events to book ahead for the Easter holidays!

activities.title: Family Craft Saturdays
activities.address: 100 London Road, Forest Hill, London SE23 3PQ
activities.description: Join the volunteer team for family craft activities inspired by the Museum's collections. This week, craft an activity to take around the gardens and explore the Nature Trail.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on crafts are great for this age. The nature trail provides an opportunity to explore outdoors.

transport.fromHackney: Overground from Hackney Downs to Forest Hill.
transport.tip: The museum is a short walk from the station.

activities.title: WordPlay: Ages 3 – 5
activities.description: Fun, interactive sessions for children aged 3-5, involving stories, songs, and rhymes.
activities.whyItWorks: These sessions engage children's imagination and language skills in a fun and interactive way.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria line to Embankment. Walk across the bridge.
transport.tip: Check the Southbank Centre website for the specific dates and times.

activities.title: Museum of London Docklands
activities.address: No.1 Warehouse, West India Quay, London, E14 4AL
activities.description: Discover London's history as a port. Interactive exhibits designed for children, with a soft play area.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of hands-on activities and a soft play area to burn off energy. Interesting historical exhibits.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the DLR to West India Quay.
transport.tip: The museum is a short walk from the DLR station.

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm
activities.description: A city farm with a variety of animals including sheep, goats, and chickens.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals! A great way to see farm animals without leaving the city.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, walk 10 mins
transport.tip: Check their website for any special events or feeding times.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A community farm with animals, gardens and a cafe.
activities.whyItWorks: A quick and easy trip for Hackney residents to see farm animals. Good for a short outing.

transport.fromHackney: Walking distance from many parts of Hackney.
transport.tip: Check their website for any special events.

activities.title: Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park
activities.description: Explore the playgrounds, fountains and green spaces of the Olympic Park.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of space to run around. The playgrounds are fantastic and there are often free events.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Hackney Wick or Stratford.
transport.tip: Check the website for events and activities.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: A museum designed specifically for children, with hands-on exhibits focused on creativity, play and design.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive and engaging exhibits perfect for young children. Encourages imagination and creativity.

transport.fromHackney: Take a bus directly to Bethnal Green or the Central line to Bethnal Green tube station.
transport.tip: Check the website for workshops and special events.

activities.title: A Squash and a Squeeze (Streaming)
activities.description: A little old lady who lives all by herself feels her house is too small. Can a wise man help?
activities.whyItWorks: A fun show that can be watched at home.

transport.fromHackney: At home
transport.tip: Watch on a rainy day.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, fascinating specimens, and interactive exhibits at one of London's most popular museums.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs! Plus lots of other animals and interactive displays to keep kids engaged.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Visit early to avoid crowds. Check for special events.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Discover space travel, technology, and transport through interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of hands-on exhibits and interactive displays. The Pattern Pod is great for younger children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Visit the Pattern Pod for sensory play. Check for shows.

activities.title: British Museum
activities.description: Explore world history and culture through fascinating artifacts and exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Activity backpacks and explorer trails make it engaging for children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria line to Tottenham Court Road or the Piccadilly line to Russell Square.
transport.tip: Pick up an activity backpack at the family desk.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: A small zoo with a variety of animals and birds. Also features a playground.
activities.whyItWorks: Free and has a variety of animals for children to see. Also has a playground.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Gospel Oak, then the bus to Golders Green station. Short walk to the park.
transport.tip: Check for feeding times and special events.

activities.title: Hampstead Heath
activities.description: Vast green space with wooded areas and open fields. Great for exploring and picnics.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of space to run around. Great for exploring nature.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Hampstead Heath.
transport.tip: Bring a picnic and enjoy the views.

activities.title: Vauxhall City Farm
activities.description: A small city farm in central London with a variety of farm animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Easy to reach and a great opportunity to see animals.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Victoria Line to Vauxhall.
transport.tip: Check for feeding times.

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: A large playground with a pirate ship theme.
activities.whyItWorks: A large and imaginative playground that will keep children entertained for hours.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria line to Notting Hill Gate. Walk through Kensington Gardens.
transport.tip: Can get busy, especially on weekends.

activities.title: Holland Park Adventure Playground
activities.description: A large playground with a variety of equipment.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a variety of activities and challenges for children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central line to Holland Park.
transport.tip: Explore the Kyoto Garden while you're there.

activities.title: Easter Eggsplorers at Hobbledown Heath
activities.description: A full-scale Easter event.
activities.whyItWorks: A good place to explore during easter.

transport.fromHackney: Tube and train to Hounslow.
transport.tip: Book in advance.

activities.title: London Zoo's Zoonormous Egg Hunt
activities.description: Search for hidden eggs around the park. Each egg reveals a secret letter and a riddle to solve and at the end there's a chocolate prize!
activities.whyItWorks: Combines a zoo visit with an easter egg hunt.

transport.fromHackney: Tube and bus to London Zoo
transport.tip: Book zoo tickets in advance.

activities.title: The Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Roam around the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun Easter themed activity in a historic setting.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Under 5s and Historic Royal Palaces members admitted free.

activities.title: Easter Egg Hunt at Ham House and Garden
activities.description: Enjoy fun activities, game stations and an exciting egg trail through this glorious garden in Richmond.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun Easter themed activity in a historic setting.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Richmond, then bus.
transport.tip: No advance booking required.

activities.title: Easter Egg Trail at Fulham Palace
activities.description: Self-guided trail weaves through both the historic palace interiors and the surrounding gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: Family-friendly activity set within one of London's oldest continually inhabited buildings.

transport.fromHackney: Tube and bus to Fulham.
transport.tip: Purchase on arrival from the museum front desk, with no advance booking required.

activities.title: Family Easter Makes Workshop at Kenwood House
activities.description: Easter themed crafts workshop.
activities.whyItWorks: Creative activity for families.

transport.fromHackney: Tube and bus to Hampstead.
transport.tip: Check website for times.

activities.title: Spring Family Fun at the Southbank Centre
activities.price: Some paid activities and some free
activities.description: A festival of fun and creativity for families at the Southbank Centre includes super shows, musical adventures, amazing puppetry and lots of free fun in the Easter break.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of variety.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria line to Embankment. Walk across the bridge.
transport.tip: Check the Southbank Centre website for the specific dates and times.

activities.title: I Want My Hat Back Trilogy
activities.description: Jon Klassen's beloved books, I Want My Hat Back, This is Not My Hat and We Found A Hat, LIVE on stage.
activities.whyItWorks: Great for younger children who love the books.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to Islington.
transport.tip: Book in advance, low availability.
